summary: Mobile app with 3 main sections (Upload, Folders, Mission). User is implementing an 'Adaptive Orbit Dock' animated navigation system at the bottom of the screen.
architectural_notes: Navigation is UI-layer only — no changes to section logic, internal structure, or functionality
Orbit Dock: 3 icons in semi-circular layout, active icon centered (scale 1.0, opacity 100%), side icons at scale 0.75–0.85, opacity 50–60%
Animation: 220–260ms ease-in-out, orbital radius ~60–70px, dock 24–32px from safe area, infinite loop swipe
Reduced motion fallback: simple horizontal slide without scale effects

existing_features.title: Create a new `OrbitDock` component (frontend/src/components/OrbitDock.tsx) implementing the 'Adaptive Orbit Dock' bottom navigation. Position three icons (Upload, Folders, Mission) on a semi-circular arc at the bottom of the screen. The active icon must always sit at the center-bottom apex of the arc (scale 1.0, opacity 100%, subtle drop-shadow/elevation). The two inactive icons must flank it symmetrically on the arc (scale 0.75–0.85, opacity 50–60%, slight brightness reduction). Semi-circle radius: 60–70px. The dock container must sit 24–32px above the bottom safe area (env(safe-area-inset-bottom)).

Implement swipe-left and swipe-right gesture handling on the OrbitDock. On swipe, icons must animate along the orbital arc path (not a flat horizontal slide) to simulate real circular motion. The transition must use an ease-in-out curve, last 220–260ms, and give a physical sense of rotation around the arc. Implement infinite loop cycling through the three items in both directions.

Add adaptive feedback to the OrbitDock active state: display a subtle glow arc or underline indicator beneath the active icon, and show a small fade-in label ('Upload', 'Folders', or 'Mission') below the active icon. When a swipe begins, the glow arc and label must fade out smoothly before the rotation animation starts. Include a light momentum effect on the swipe motion.

Add professional micro-interactions to each dock icon: (1) Upload icon performs a very subtle pulse animation every 8–10 seconds when it is not being interacted with. (2) Folders icon displays a subtle layered-depth visual effect (e.g., a gentle shadow stack). (3) Mission icon plays a very subtle motion micro-animation when it becomes the active center icon. All micro-interactions must be minimal and non-playful.

Implement reduced-motion mode support in the OrbitDock. When the user's OS/browser has 'prefers-reduced-motion: reduce' set, fall back to a simple horizontal slide transition between icons with no scale transformation and no orbital arc motion. Reuse the existing useReducedMotion hook (frontend/src/hooks/useReducedMotion.ts).

Integrate the OrbitDock into the existing HomePage (frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx) and CircularBottomNavigation area, replacing or wrapping the current bottom navigation rendering. The OrbitDock must trigger the exact same section-switching logic (Upload / Folders / Mission) that the existing navigation triggers. No backend logic, data queries, upload flows, folder operations, or mission operations may be altered. Only the visual navigation UI layer changes.

new_feature_requests.summary: Adaptive Orbit Dock: semi-circular animated bottom navigation for Upload, Folders, Mission with swipe orbital motion, scale/opacity effects, glow arc feedback, labels, micro-interactions, and reduced motion fallback
